meaning: adj.空想的,虚构的,被特别爱好的
examples: She fancied (that) she could hear footsteps.
He started to chat to me and I could tell that he really fancied himself.
He fancied it up with renovations and restorations in 2004, and is now seeking a buyer.
In fact, this congenial chap always fancied himself as the consummate soft-hearted, social minded sort.
